summary: expansion-tracker.json next_id stale: next_id=238 while disk contains EXP-238 through EXP-242. Also: ISS-1496.assigned_exp='EXP-240' collides with existing EXP-240-WIN069-circuity-citation-retraction-iss1487-iss1488.json (created 2026-04-23). Root cause inferred, not investigated further in this run.
detail: During ISS-1496 work this run, the analyst found that monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json had next_id=238 but the expansions/ directory on disk held EXP-238, EXP-239, EXP-240, EXP-241, EXP-242 — all registered to other issues across Apr 23 decider runs. This means: (a) the tracker did NOT grow with disk writes for five consecutive decider-side allocations (EXP-238..242), and (b) the decider itself assigned 'EXP-240' to ISS-1496 (at 2026-04-24T16:01:01Z per assigned_at) despite the EXP-240 file already existing for WIN-069. If the analyst had honored the assigned_exp literally, our EXP-240 output would have overwritten the WIN-069 citation retraction work. This run defensively allocated EXP-243 (disk-max+1) and bumped next_id to 244. Issue-proposal proposal-ISS-1496-resolution.json documents the same.
diagnostic_checks_recommended: Confirm whether EXP-238..242 were registered under different IDs in the tracker (check for any items with status ready-for-integration referring to WIN-010/025/061/068/069 created 2026-04-23 that match these files).
If EXP-238..242 are absent from the tracker, decide whether to back-fill them or accept the gap as 'disk is the source of truth' and the tracker is purely a status index for pending work.
Trace the decider step (reference: EXP-234 → EXP-242 decider runs on 2026-04-23) that writes expansion files — identify where the tracker update is missing.
Check that the decider's assigned_exp allocation code reads from max(tracker.next_id, disk_max) rather than raw tracker.next_id, to prevent future collisions.
blast_radius_if_ignored: Next decider run that honors tracker.next_id and writes an expansion will collide with an existing file. If assigned_exp fields are used literally by the analyst, more collisions are possible. The integrity_gate (pre-push) may or may not catch this — it depends on whether the gate checks for existing file overwrite.
mitigations_already_applied: This run allocated EXP-243 (not EXP-238/239/240/241/242/238 which would have collided) and bumped tracker.next_id from 238 to 244.
proposal-ISS-1496-resolution.json flags the assigned_exp=EXP-240 collision for decider reconciliation.
do_not: Do NOT blindly trust tracker.next_id in future allocation code paths without cross-checking against disk.
Do NOT close this notice by merely reconciling ISS-1496.assigned_exp — the underlying allocation bug deserves a trace.
